library.overview: The college library is a central knowledge hub available to all students, faculty, and staff. Most college libraries are open 6 days a week (Monday–Saturday) and some have digital 24/7 access.

timings.weekdays: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM (general); extended to 10 PM during exam seasons
timings.exam_season: Extended hours notified through library notice board / student portal

services: Book lending (2-5 books per student for 7-14 days)
Reference section (books for in-library use only)
Digital library / e-resources (NPTEL, DELNET, INFLIBNET/N-LIST access)
Competitive exam section (UPSC, GATE, CAT books)
library.membership: Valid college ID card is mandatory for library membership and book borrowing.
library.fine_policy: Overdue fine: Rs. 1-5 per book per day. Lost book: student pays replacement cost + Rs. 100 fine.
library.n_list_access: INFLIBNET N-LIST provides access to 6000+ e-journals and 3,35,000+ e-books. Access through library credentials on nlist.inflibnet.ac.in
library.how_to_access_ebook: Login to your college's N-LIST/DELNET portal using student ID. Access from campus WiFi for free.
library.book_reservation: Popular books can be reserved online or through the library desk. You'll be notified when the book is available.

hostel.overview: On-campus hostels provide affordable residential accommodation for outstation students. Separate hostels for boys and girls.
room_types: Single occupancy (for senior students / extra fee)
facilities_in_hostel: Visitor's room (for meeting guests during designated hours)

timings.entry_curfew: 10:00 PM – 10:30 PM for girls; 11:00 PM – 12:00 AM for boys (varies by institution)
timings.visiting_hours: Usually 4:00 PM – 7:00 PM on weekdays; 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM on weekends (family only)

hostel.allotment_process: Fill hostel application form online or at the hostel office. Allotment based on merit/first-come-first-serve. Pay hostel fee and submit documents.
rules: Maintain silence during study hours (10 PM – 6 AM)
No consumption of alcohol, tobacco or drugs
Cooking in rooms is strictly prohibited
Guests must be registered at the hostel office
Any electrical appliances must be declared to warden
Abide by curfew timings; late entries require prior permission
Ragging in any form is a criminal offence (UGC Regulation 2009)
hostel.warden_contact: Contact the Chief Warden or Block Warden for hostel-related issues. Emergency: security desk at hostel gate.
hostel.fee_general: Rs. 10,000 – Rs. 60,000 per year depending on room type and institution (includes mess charges in most cases)

canteen_cafeteria.overview: Campus canteens provide affordable, hygienic meals and snacks to students and faculty throughout the day.
canteen_cafeteria.typical_timings: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM (main canteen); Small stalls: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM
canteen_cafeteria.menu_type: Vegetarian and non-vegetarian options; South Indian, North Indian, Chinese, Continental in larger campuses
canteen_cafeteria.average_cost: Meals: Rs. 40-80; Snacks: Rs. 10-30; Tea/Coffee: Rs. 5-15
canteen_cafeteria.food_committee: Student Food Committee monitors quality and hygiene. Report issues to committee or mess/canteen warden.
canteen_cafeteria.special_dietary: Jain food, diabetic meals available on request in many colleges. Inform the canteen manager in advance.

sports_complex.overview: College sports facilities promote physical fitness and represent the institution in inter-college and national competitions.
sports_complex.timings: Morning: 6:00 AM – 8:00 AM; Evening: 4:00 PM – 7:00 PM
sports_complex.registration: Register with the Physical Education (PE) department to access sports facilities and join teams.
sports_complex.inter_college: College sports teams participate in inter-college tournaments, university games, and AIU (Association of Indian Universities) championships.
sports_complex.sports_quota: Sports quota admissions available in many colleges. Outstation training and leave granted to state/national level sports persons.

medical_center.overview: College health centers / dispensaries provide first-aid, basic medical consultations, and health emergency support.
medical_center.timings: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM on weekdays; Emergency support 24/7 through security desk
medical_center.emergency: For medical emergencies, contact the security desk or medical officer immediately. Ambulance available at most campuses.
medical_center.tie_up_hospitals: Most colleges have tie-up with nearby hospitals for advanced treatment. Students may get concession.
medical_center.mental_health: Many UGC-compliant colleges now have counseling services for academic stress, anxiety, and personal issues. Strictly confidential.
medical_center.health_insurance: Some institutions provide group health insurance to students. Check with the student affairs office.

placement_cell.overview: The Training & Placement (T&P) Cell is the bridge between students and industry. It coordinates campus recruitment drives, internships, and career development.
services: Higher education guidance (GATE, GRE, CAT, IELTS prep)

placement_process.pre_placement: Register on placement portal → Attend pre-placement talks → Submit updated resume
placement_process.aptitude_test: Online/offline aptitude test (Verbal, Quantitative, Logical Reasoning, Technical)
placement_process.interviews: Technical interview → HR interview (sometimes Group Discussion in between)
placement_process.offer_letter: Selected candidates receive offer letter; must respond within deadline

eligibility_criteria.cgpa: Most companies require minimum 6.0-7.5 CGPA; some require 8.0+
eligibility_criteria.backlogs: Zero active backlogs typically required; some companies allow cleared backlogs
eligibility_criteria.gaps: Explain any academic gaps honestly during interview

placement_cell.placement_statistics: Placement statistics (% placed, average/highest packages) are published annually by college. Available on college website.
placement_cell.contact: Visit T&P cell with your updated resume. Register early in final year. Attend all placement preparation sessions.

computer_labs.overview: Campus computer laboratories provide access to computing resources, software tools, and high-speed internet.
computer_labs.timings: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM (regular); may extend to 8 PM during project season
computer_labs.software_available: MATLAB, AutoCAD, Simulink, ANSYS, Tally, Adobe suite, SPSS, Scilab, Oracle, MySQL, Python, Java IDEs, C/C++ compilers — varies by college type
computer_labs.internet_access: High-speed internet (10 Mbps – 1 Gbps) available in labs; many campuses have campus-wide WiFi
computer_labs.printing: Printing and scanning available in labs on chargeable basis (Rs. 2-5 per page)

library_digital.nptel: NPTEL (National Programme on Technology Enhanced Learning) — Free video lectures from IIT/IISc faculty on swayam.gov.in and nptel.ac.in
library_digital.swayam: SWAYAM platform offers free MOOCs. UGC-approved credit transfer for completed SWAYAM courses.
library_digital.coursera_edx: Premium platforms; many colleges have institutional subscriptions
library_digital.ieee_acm: Digital library access to IEEE Xplore, ACM Digital Library through INFLIBNET in participating colleges

transport.overview: College transport services connect the campus with major city/town areas.
transport.bus_pass: Students can apply for a monthly/semester bus pass at the transport office. Subsidized rates for regular routes.
transport.timing: Morning pick-up: 7:00 AM – 8:30 AM (drops at campus); Evening drop: 4:30 PM – 6:00 PM
transport.route_info: Route charts available at the transport office, notice boards, and college website/app.
transport.railway_concession: Full-time students are eligible for 50-75% concession on railway tickets for travel to home town. Apply through the college with bonafide certificate.

wifi_internet.overview: Campus WiFi is provided to all students free of charge after registration.
wifi_internet.how_to_connect: Register your device with the IT department or student portal. Login using student credentials.
wifi_internet.bandwidth: Varies: 50 Mbps to 10 Gbps depending on institution (IITs have dedicated NMEICT 1 Gbps+ links)
wifi_internet.policy: Academic use only. Torrenting, streaming excessive videos, and illegal downloads are prohibited and monitored.
wifi_internet.it_support: Contact IT support desk for WiFi issues: password reset, device registration, VPN access.

bank_atm.overview: Most college campuses have branch/extension counter or ATM of nationalized banks.
bank_atm.services: Account opening for fresh students, educational loan processing, demand draft, ATM
bank_atm.scholarship_accounts: Scholarship disbursements often made directly to student bank accounts linked with Aadhar.

student_center.overview: Student Activity Centre / Student Centre is the hub for all extracurricular activities.
contains: College cooperative store (books, stationery at discount)

auditorium_seminar_halls.auditorium: Main auditorium capacity: 500-2000 persons; used for convocations, cultural fests, major seminars
auditorium_seminar_halls.seminar_halls: Seminar halls: 50-200 capacity; used for departmental events, guest lectures, workshops
auditorium_seminar_halls.booking: Book through the college administration office or student affairs department. Departments get priority; student clubs apply through coordinator.
